diagrams.title: Transmission Modes Types
diagrams.description: A visual comparison of the three transmission modes: Simplex, Half-Duplex, and Full-Duplex. Shows the direction of data flow using arrows between two communication endpoints (Device A and Device B). Simplex shows one-way arrow, Half-Duplex shows bidirectional but alternating arrows, Full-Duplex shows simultaneous two-way arrows.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental difference between Simplex (one-way), Half-Duplex (two-way but not simultaneous), and Full-Duplex (two-way simultaneous) communication, which is essential for understanding network performance and device capabilities.

diagrams.title: Network Devices and Transmission Modes
diagrams.description: A diagram showing common network devices aligned with the transmission modes they typically use. Displays devices like Keyboard (Simplex), Walkie-Talkie (Half-Duplex), and Telephone/Modern Network Switch (Full-Duplex). Each device is placed under its corresponding transmission mode category with visual icons.
diagrams.purpose: Helps students connect theoretical transmission modes to real-world devices and understand why certain devices use specific modes for optimal operation.

diagrams.title: Full-Duplex vs Half-Duplex Network Communication
diagrams.description: A detailed comparison diagram showing a network scenario with a Switch (Full-Duplex) versus a Hub (Half-Duplex). The switch diagram shows two computers sending data simultaneously in both directions without collision. The hub diagram shows computers taking turns to transmit, with collision detection indicators.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the practical advantage of Full-Duplex switches over Half-Duplex hubs in network communication, showing why modern networks use Full-Duplex for better performance and collision avoidance.
